query: How active is the 2026 US tornado season and what is the forecast/outlook for early July 2026 severe weather? Current ENSO state (La Nina/El Nino) for summer 2026 and any Climate Prediction Center outlook for July 2026 severe convective weather in northern Plains and Midwest.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**1. 2026 Tornado Season — Very active early, near-normal projected finish**
- 500+ tornadoes and 78 deaths through mid-April 2026 (NOAA SPC preliminary)
- March was historic: ~204 tornadoes (~3× average, 3rd most active March on record)
- An EF5 hit Wells County, Indiana (March 10) — only the 2nd US EF5 since 2013
- Full-year projection: 1,050–1,250 tornadoes (near/slightly below the ~1,225 average)
- Damage concentrated in IL/IN/MI (eastward shift toward the Midwest)

**2. Early July 2026 Severe Weather — Active in northern Plains/Upper Midwest**
- July 1–2: SPC issued **Enhanced Risk (3/5)** for the Upper Midwest, Slight Risk from the Dakotas into the western Great Lakes; 80 mph winds, large hail, a few tornadoes
- Week 1–2 July: CPC outlook shifts emphasis to **extreme heat** (heat dome), with severe convection expected mainly along the ridge's northern periphery rather than as an organized outbreak

**3. ENSO State — Key correction**
- Despite La Niña earlier in 2026, the **current state is El Niño (Advisory), present and strengthening**
- Niño-3.4: +0.7°C (CPC weekly); IRI showed ~+0.94°C (May) and ~+1.7°C weekly (mid-June)
- Forecast to intensify into winter 2026–27; **63% chance of a very strong El Niño** in Nov–Jan
- The La Niña→El Niño transition is noted as an analog to the deadly 1974 and 2011 seasons (context, not a forecast)

One caveat worth flagging for the forecasting caller: some secondary aggregator sources had internal inconsistencies (e.g., a "Minnesota EF5" headline vs. the Indiana EF5 in its own text), so the state-level EF5 attribution should be treated with mild caution, though the March 10 Indiana EF5 is consistently detailed.

sources.title: Tornadoes of 2026: 500+ Tornadoes, 78 Deaths, and the First EF5 in Minnesota History
sources.snippet: Through April 14: 500+ tornadoes, 78 deaths; March 204 tornadoes (3rd most active); EF5 Wells County, Indiana March 10; full-year forecast 1,050-1,250.

sources.title: Climate Prediction Center: ENSO Diagnostic Discussion
sources.snippet: El Niño conditions present; weekly Niño-3.4 +0.7°C; forecast to intensify into winter 2026-27 with 63% chance of very strong El Niño in Nov-Jan.

sources.title: IRI ENSO Forecast — June 2026 Quick Look
sources.snippet: El Niño conditions strengthening; Niño-3.4 +0.48°C (MAM), +0.94°C (May), ~+1.7°C weekly June 17, 2026. Published June 22, 2026.

sources.title: Enhanced Risk issued for severe thunderstorms across Midwest and Upper Great Lakes
sources.snippet: July 1, 2026 Enhanced Risk for Upper Midwest/Great Lakes; winds to 80 mph, large hail, few tornadoes; storms initiating over southern Minnesota.

sources.title: SPC Day 1 Convective Outlook — Jul 02, 2026
sources.snippet: Enhanced Risk for portions of the Upper Midwest; Slight Risk from the Dakotas into the western Great Lakes and over the southern Appalachians/Tennessee Valley.

sources.title: CPC US Hazards Outlook, valid July 9-15, 2026
sources.snippet: Rebuilding mid-level ridge; slight risk of extreme heat across Great Plains into Upper Midwest Week 2; significant heat event Week 1 (July 4-8).

sources.title: SPC Day 4-8 Severe Weather Outlook
sources.snippet: Day 4-8 severe outlook product covering early-to-mid July 2026.

key_facts: 2026 US tornado season: 500+ tornadoes and 78 deaths through mid-April 2026 (NOAA SPC preliminary); full-year projection 1,050-1,250, near/slightly below the ~1,225 average.
March 2026 had ~204 tornadoes, nearly 3x the ~75-80 average and the 3rd most active March on record.
An EF5 struck Wells County, Indiana on March 10, 2026 (200+ mph) — only the 2nd US EF5 since 2013, following the June 2025 Enderlin, ND EF5.
Damage concentrated in Illinois/Indiana/Michigan, reinforcing an eastward shift from the Great Plains toward the Midwest/Dixie Alley.

July 1-2, 2026: SPC issued Enhanced Risk (level 3/5) for the Upper Midwest with Slight Risk from the Dakotas into the western Great Lakes; hazards include 80 mph winds, large hail, and a few tornadoes.

CPC Hazards Outlook (issued July 1, 2026) emphasizes extreme heat: a significant central/eastern US heat event Week 1 (July 4-8) and slight extreme-heat risk across the Plains into the Upper Midwest Week 2 (July 9-15), with no organized severe convective hazard highlighted at Week-2 range.
Current ENSO state (June 2026 CPC advisory) is El Niño conditions PRESENT and strengthening — an El Niño Advisory — after La Niña transitioned to neutral in Feb-Apr 2026.
Latest weekly Niño-3.4 index +0.7°C (CPC); IRI June 22, 2026 update showed ~+0.94°C May mean and ~+1.7°C weekly on June 17, 2026.
El Niño forecast to intensify into winter 2026-27 with a 63% chance of a very strong event in Nov-Jan 2026-27, potentially among the largest since 1950.
The 2026 La Niña-to-El Niño transition is cited as an analog to the deadly 1974 and 2011 tornado seasons (context, not a forecast).

data_points.metric: Probability of very strong El Niño Nov-Jan 2026-27
SPC severe risk level for Upper Midwest, July 2, 2026
Peak damaging wind gust potential, July 1-2, 2026 Midwest
